title: ETS set table type — hash-based storage
purpose: The `set` table type is the default and most common ETS table. It stores tuples keyed by a specified element position (default: first element). Keys are unique — inserting a tuple with an existing key overwrites the previous entry. Lookup is O(1) via hashing. This is the foundation that bag, duplicate_bag, and most ETS BIFs build on.
task: Implement `EtsSet` as a concrete implementation of the `EtsTable` trait using a `DashMap` for concurrent hash-based storage.

requirements.title: EtsSet implementation
requirements.spec: Implement `EtsSet` as a struct wrapping `DashMap<Term, Term>` (key → full tuple) with the EtsTable trait.
acceptance: `EtsSet::new(metadata: EtsTableMetadata) -> EtsSet`
`insert(tuple)`: extracts key at `keypos`, inserts/overwrites in DashMap
`lookup(key)`: returns vec of 0 or 1 matching tuples (set = unique keys)
`delete_key(key)`: removes entry, returns true if existed
`tab2list()`: returns all tuples (unordered)
Key extraction uses `keypos` from metadata (1-based → 0-based index into tuple elements)
Non-tuple insert returns EtsError::Badarg
Test: insert {a, 1}, lookup a → [{a, 1}]. Insert {a, 2}, lookup a → [{a, 2}] (overwritten)

requirements.title: Term hashing for ETS keys
requirements.spec: ETS keys are arbitrary terms. Implement term hashing that is consistent with term equality — equal terms must hash to the same value.
acceptance: `term_hash(term: Term) -> u64` produces a deterministic hash
Atoms hash by atom index
Integers hash by value
Binaries hash by content
Tuples hash by recursive element hashing
Lists hash by recursive element hashing
Equal terms (per term comparison) produce equal hashes
Test: term_hash(atom(ok)) == term_hash(atom(ok))
Test: term_hash(42) == term_hash(42)
Test: different terms produce different hashes (probabilistic, test a variety)

requirements.title: EtsSet registered as table type
requirements.spec: Wire EtsSet into the table creation path so `ets:new(Name, [set])` produces an EtsSet.
acceptance: Table creation with `EtsTableType::Set` instantiates an `EtsSet`
The EtsSet is wrapped in `Arc<dyn EtsTable>` and stored in the registry
Test: create set table, insert, lookup — round-trips correctly through the registry

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement bag or duplicate_bag semantics — those are B-092
SHALL NOT implement ordered iteration — that's ordered_set in B-093
SHALL NOT implement match specifications — that's B-096/B-097
SHALL use DashMap (already in Cargo.toml) for the underlying storage
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
